en: I've got these glasses in because these were from the original Blade Runner, this is what they used.
So I thought, you know, attention to detail, so fans will probably like that.
That's so cool that you did that. Are you a fan of the originals? I've never seen it.
I appreciate your candy.
Bleak, dystopian, an absolute nightmare to be honest with you, that's just my interviewing techniques.
Let's talk about the movie, Blade Runner 2049. Cheer up.
Well you know, it's a bit of a bleak day.
Yeah, no it's not, it's a lovely day in London, let's keep it that way.
I'm so sorry. This is not the introduction we were promised.
Do you want me to pick it up a bit? Bring me on with a little happier music.
Well there's so much secrecy around this new film, what can you tell me about it?
Obviously you've told me that you're having a drink.
I feel like that's where this is headed.
Harrison, when you got that call to say, listen, we're making another Blade Runner and we want
you to be in it, what was your reaction? So what?
In theory. Help yourselves.
I notice there's nothing left for me.
I needed that, I'm warmed up.
Ridley said, would you be interested? I said.
How much? Show me the money.
No, I said, show me the script. Anyway.
Shall we talk to Ryan? Yeah, talk to him.
Did you have much fun on the set? Yeah, it was great fun.
Did you get your knitting needles out, because I know you like to relax. Oh, we're going to go there.
We're going to go with the knitting. Oh, it's going to be like that.
I just looked at your jumper and I thought, did you knit that?
This is what I did on set. I knitted this sweater. It's a nice sweater.
It's Blade Runner inspired. I don't know what to say now. I don't know.
Is this an interview or is this? All right, I'll go with you, Harrison.
You're my man. You're my man tonight. Get out of the way. Thank you. Do you guys need help with the cameras?
Come back.
35 years since the role. How did you find the stunts?
I found them extremely difficult, which accounts for the fact that I hit this guy one time.
That's show business. The story is that I missed him 99 times and I hit him once.
You know, they say don't meet your heroes. I would say don't get punched by them.
Turns out it doesn't matter who you're getting punched by. It's still just getting punched.
You've obviously been in Blade Runner, a sequel to one of Harrison's films.
Would you like to maybe be in a sequel to one of his films? Maybe La La La La Land?
Can't dance. You can't dance? Can't sing. Really?
That's not true. Can't play the piano. There's the voice of an angel. It's like an angel pouring honey in your ear.
We're working on our harmonies right now. Are you serious? Yeah. Go for it.
We're going to keep working on them.
Can I just go back and talk about... Moulding things. Creating.
Can I just go back and talk about... Natural elements. Sorry. Is this a bad time?
Yeah, just... Beautiful day, huh? Thank you.
There is a darkness and there is a dystopian atmosphere.
But there is also a triumph of the human spirit.
I mean, that's... Alright. What can you add to that? Is there a mic I can drop?
Well, it's lovely to meet you both. Nice to meet you too. Thank you so much.
So nice to meet you. Funniest interview ever. What a day.
What a way to start the day. This morning?
This morning. Yeah. Cheers. What a morning.
You know I don't drink.
